questions.question: Explain the primary difference between horizontal and vertical scaling in cloud computing.
keyPoints: Horizontal scaling (scaling out) adds more instances of the same type to a pool of resources.
Vertical scaling (scaling up) increases the capacity of an existing resource, such as adding more CPU or RAM to a single server.
Horizontal scaling is generally preferred in cloud architectures for its flexibility and lack of downtime.

questions.question: Describe the architectural concept of object-based storage and list two key advantages it holds over traditional block storage for cloud environments.
keyPoints: Object storage manages data as objects (data, metadata, and a unique identifier) instead of as files in folders or blocks on a disk.
It is highly scalable and flat, allowing for massive data growth across distributed systems.

Advantages include: virtually unlimited scalability; built-in metadata for improved data management; better suited for unstructured data; and higher durability and availability through data distribution and replication.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the architectural principles and typical use cases for IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS delivery models. Provide a specific example of a workload suited for each.
keyPoints: IaaS provides raw compute, storage, and networking resources. The user manages the OS, runtime, and applications. Example: Hosting a customizable web server or a complex multi-tier application.

PaaS provides a platform and environment for developers to build and deploy applications. The cloud provider manages the underlying infrastructure. Example: Developing and deploying a web application using a specific programming language and framework.

SaaS delivers a complete, functional application over the internet. The provider manages everything from infrastructure to the application itself. Example: Using a web-based email service like Gmail or a CRM like Salesforce.
The key contrast is the level of abstraction and management responsibility, which shifts from the user (IaaS) to the provider (SaaS).
